verdict_reason: Handlers correctly satisfy the 400-on-malformed-JSON and error-on-ASB-failure ACs with safe generic bodies and tests, but the error contract diverges from the documented ProblemDetail+traceId pattern (Major) and lacks a catch-all handler (Minor)

todos.issue: Error responses are ad-hoc Map.of("error", ...) bodies instead of RFC 7807 ProblemDetail with a /problems/... type URI and traceId — the repo's RequestTracingFilter puts traceId in MDC but it is never surfaced in error responses, and this PR establishes the error contract for the whole new service
todos.fix: Return ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(...) from both handlers, set problem.setType(URI.create("/problems/...")) and problem.setProperty("traceId", MDC.get("traceId")) per the documented GlobalExceptionHandler pattern

todos.issue: No catch-all @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) — unexpected exceptions fall through to Spring Boot's default error attributes, giving a different error body shape from the {"error": ...} responses defined here
todos.fix: Add a handleException(Exception ex) handler that logs at error level and returns 500 with the same body shape ("An unexpected error occurred"), matching the documented GlobalExceptionHandler pattern
